content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

I accept all four amendments. Two are confirmed contract mismatches against the current `br` CLI/JSON behavior, and two are deterministic idempotency failures in `materialize_beads()` during realistic reruns.

## Votes

- `fr-3-e76b2f84`: Accept
  `BrCommand::show()` always runs `br show <id> --json`, `exec_json()` deserializes directly into the requested type, and `BeadDetail` is a plain struct rather than an array wrapper. This repo already has separate `BrShowResponse` enums in other callers precisely because `br show --json` can return an array, and local verification here shows `br show ralph-burning-9ni.4.6 --json` returns `array` on `br 0.1.35`. `materialize_beads()` calls `exec_json::<BeadDetail>` on both create-resolution and reuse paths, so this will raise `BrParseError` in real runs. The tests mask the bug by mocking `show` as an object payload instead of the real array shape.
- `fr-3-6bfeb81e`: Accept
  `list_all()` feeds `find_existing_materialized_bead()` all milestone-scoped beads, including closed and deferred ones, but both `summary_matches_materialized_bead_except_role()` and `detail_matches_materialized_bead()` only accept `Open | InProgress`. That means a rerun after normal milestone progress will fail as soon as a previously exported bead is closed or deferred: with only a non-active match it errors as `non-active status`, and with an active match plus historical duplicate it errors as ambiguous. For a command that is explicitly designed to reuse previously exported beads and support reruns, that is a real idempotency/incremental-export defect. Any fix needs to address both the summary and detail status filters.
- `fr-3-8f748c9e`: Accept
  `BrCommand::kv()` renders flags as `--key=value`, so `BrMutationAdapter::create_bead()` currently emits `--label=<value>`. With the installed `br 0.1.35`, local verification shows `br create --label=alpha --dry-run` fails at argument parsing with `unexpected argument '--label'`, while `-l alpha` and `--labels=...` are accepted forms. `materialized_bead_labels()` always injects at least `milestone:<id>`, so every real `materialize_beads()` create attempt will fail before any bead is created. The existing tests miss this because they assert the builder shape and use a mock runner instead of the actual CLI contract.
- `fr-3-f08e0ba4`: Accept
  The bundle validator only rejects duplicate titles for non-explicit proposals; proposals with explicit IDs can legitimately share a title. `materialize_beads()` also maintains a canonical proposal-id map for dependency resolution, so the export model already recognizes proposal identity as distinct from title. But `find_existing_materialized_bead()` ignores that identity and keys reuse purely on normalized title plus type/labels/role. After a successful first export of two same-title explicit proposals, the second run will see two active milestone-scoped matches and fail on `eligible_indices.len() > 1`, because no stable proposal-to-bead marker is persisted or consulted. That is a real idempotency bug unless validation is tightened to forbid this shape or export stores a canonical proposal identifier on each created bead.
